channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Debugging a Python "Memory Leak" That Was Actually a Measurement Bug (ru_maxrss vs VmRSS)
item.description: I was running a pipeline for building a RAG knowledge base: crawl web articles, split them into chunks, create embeddings, and push them into Qdrant. Then my memory usage logs started looking like...

item.title: Docker Compose for Local Development: Complete Setup (2026)
item.description: git clone → make dev → working environment with Postgres, Redis, and email testing, same for every developer. That's the goal. The docker-compose.yml services: app: build: context: . dockerfile:...

item.title: Go To Do Rust
item.description: In this post, I want to go from step by step what it would look like building a taskr binary in both Go and Rust side by side so you can see conversion conventions and why Rust wins in the battle...

item.title: Monitor risky agents and keep agent governance current
item.description: Agent governance does not finish when inventory, custom security attributes, Conditional Access policies, access packages, and lifecycle workflows are configured. That is only the foundation. The...

item.title: Installing GBase 8a Cluster with a Non‑Default SSH Port
item.description: If your Linux servers use a custom SSH port (e.g., 2222 instead of 22), a standard GBase 8a cluster installation will fail because the installer and internal services cannot reach each other. This...

item.title: Show HN: LastShelf – an emergency map of your family's documents bills& contacts
item.description: After my father was diagnosed with Stage 3 kidney cancer, my family was thrown into a tailspin. Getting second opinions, planning surgery, ensuring insurance coverage, coping with the fear. It was...

item.title: Governing AI agents with Microsoft Entra Agent ID and Agent 365
item.description: AI agents are becoming part of the enterprise identity landscape. They can be created across different platforms, access corporate data, call APIs, participate in workflows, and in some cases...

item.title: Open-ended delegation froze 8 of 13 people. Here's what unfroze them
item.description: TL;DR: "Do it however you like" only helps people who can already picture their first move. To everyone else it reads as fog. That one instruction left 8 of my 13 workshop participants stuck at...

item.title: "Back" to move forward
item.description: Sometimes, it’s essential to return to the beginning to clear your mind. Last month, I took a 15-day vacation, and I really needed to reset my thoughts. Working as a QA requires a great deal of...

item.title: TLS certificates for internal services done right
item.description: Article URL: https://tuxnet.dev/posts/tls-for-internal-services/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48846995 Points: 12 # Comments: 3

item.title: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
item.description: Claude’s new Reflect dashboard doesn’t just visualize how you use AI. It also subtly reinforces how much of your daily work now depends on Anthropic’s chatbot.

item.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are bigger than the last 25 years of tech exits
item.description: Three big AI IPOs are set to generate more value than all the U.S. VC-backed exits since 2000.

item.title: Sonos Ace wireless headphones are steeply discounted
item.description: If you’re looking for a discount on a great pair of active noise-canceling headphones for your summer adventures (even if they’re indoors), you’re in luck. The Sonos Ace headphones in black are...

item.title: The PocketMage resurrects the PDA with an e-paper screen
item.description: Personal digital assistants like the iconic Palm Pilot were one of many devices we thought went extinct with the arrival of the smartphone. But similar to Canon resurrecting a nearly decade-old...

item.title: Sony brings back the superzoom RX10 with a stacked sensor and a high price
item.description: Sony is bringing back the RX10 superzoom camera after a nearly nine-year gap between models. The newly announced RX10 V retains the same 24-600mm equivalent f/2.4-4 Zeiss Vario-Sonnar 25x zoom...

item.title: Comcast is breaking up with NBCU. Why did it ever buy it in the first place?
item.description: Today on Decoder, I’m talking with Peter Kafka, who is chief correspondent at Business Insider and host of Channels, a podcast about the media industry. And it’s a big week for the media industry...

item.title: Meta says its new AI model is ready to compete on coding
item.description: After reentering the AI race with its first in-house Muse Spark model in April, Meta is now opening up the doors to developers with a new model that can plug into AI coding software with the new...

item.title: ICE agents are making house calls for online critics
item.description: A few hours after checking into a hotel in New York City, David Streever woke up to a call from the front desk saying someone was looking for him. Streever had just landed on a return trip from...

item.title: Say hello to Claude Wrapped
item.description: The popularity of Spotify Wrapped has kicked off a wide range of year-in-review features, on apps from YouTube to Uber - and now, the lookback trend has come to AI. Anthropic on Thursday announced...

item.title: Character.AI wants a piece of the microdrama pie
item.description: Character.AI's plan to become more than just an LLM-powered chatbot platform is going beyond interactive books, comics, and audio dramas. Today, the company announced the debut of c.ai Series -...

item.title: Popular open source AI developer tool Ollama raises $65M, grows to nearly 9M users
item.description: Benchmark-backed Ollama has amassed 176,000 stars, and nearly 17,000 forks on GitHub by helping developers easily run AI on their PCs.

item.title: Character.AI enters the microdrama arena with its own productions, but there’s a twist
item.description: In an interesting twist that takes advantage of the company's core product, users can chat with these shows' characters, ask them questions, and even roleplay different storylines.

item.title: FL Studio 2026 turns its AI chatbot into your assistant engineer
item.description: Last year, Image Line introduced Gopher for FL Studio, an AI chatbot that was basically a glorified instruction manual. You asked it how to do something, and it would serve up the relevant...

item.title: AI Attacks Move in Minutes. Join This Webinar on Building a Defense That Keeps Up
item.description: AI has changed how fast attacks move. Work that once took an attacker days now takes minutes. Using models like Mythos, attackers write tailored bait, pick targets, test what lands, and jump to...

item.title: The Download: a nuclear landmark, and China eyes Nvidia chips
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Four nuclear reactors hit a big milestone in the US —Casey...

item.title: Nandan Nilekani leaves GP role at Fundamentum as it launches $200M third fund
item.description: Nilekani remains Fundamentum's anchor investor as the firm expands its leadership team and targets AI and fintech startups in India.

item.title: Starlink deployments on record pace
item.description: SpaceX is currently ahead of last year's record-setting pace for Starlink satellite deployments. SpaceX launched 1,589 Starlink satellites into low-Earth orbit in the first half of 2026, according...

item.title: The Language of AI Could Change How Humans Speak
item.description: Because of the way they are trained, large language models capture only a slice of human language. They’re trained on the written word, from textbooks to social media posts, and our speech as...

item.title: Summer of Clearinghouses
item.description: Everyone seems to have announced a clearinghouse over the past few weeks. We did too. Ours is called Athena, and the main thing that sets it apart is that it was already real and running when we...

item.title: GodDamn Ransomware Uses PoisonX Driver to Disable Endpoint Defenses
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new ransomware family called GodDamn that employs the PoisonX kernel driver to neutralize security software as part of its defense evasion strategy....
